title: Nervilia petraea
intro: Nervilia petraea, also known as the rock nerve orchid, is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to Southeast Asia. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Nervilia petraea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Nervilia petraea is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often in combination with other herbs, to treat a range of ailments.
taxonomy: Nervilia petraea is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification places it within the subfamily Orchidoideae. Further, it is placed within the tribe Nervilieae. Within the genus Nervilia, it is one of the species known to exist. Nervilia petraea is a terrestrial orchid native to tropical Asia.
